summary: The PR changes the overdue-reminder script in AGENTS.md/HEARTBEAT.md but leaves the canonical reminder behavior inconsistent across other prompt/spec docs. That prevents a clean shame-safety validation for missed reminders and should be reconciled before merge.

blocking_issues.message: This PR changes the missed-reminder script in AGENTS.md and HEARTBEAT.md to a shorter "This was due a bit ago" pattern, but the canonical user-facing specs still define missed reminders as "deliver with apology" plus an explicit low-pressure choice to "handle it now or reschedule" in docs/user-interactions.md:794 and docs/task-lifecycle.md:944. Because these prompt/spec files are executable behavior, the repo now contains two different overdue-reminder contracts. That inconsistency is blocking under the review procedure, and it also makes the shame-safety requirement impossible to validate cleanly against docs/ai-prompts.md:66-75 and design/adhd-priorities.md:39-43, which require consistent nonjudgmental wording and safe exit ramps.

fix_suggestions.patch_hint: Choose one overdue-reminder pattern and propagate it across all reminder-spec files. If the new shorter copy is intended, update docs/user-interactions.md and docs/task-lifecycle.md to match and preserve an explicit shame-safe exit ramp; if not, revert AGENTS.md/HEARTBEAT.md to the existing canonical wording.
